title: General Crime Victim Assistance * Older Adults - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > General Crime Victim Assistance * Older Adults 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF WASHINGTON COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE ELDER SAFE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps older adults after they report a crime and continues to help them through the criminal justice process.Volunteer victim advocates provide crime victims with emotional support and information relating to the criminal justice system and their case. Assistance may include personal support, court advocacy, or help filling out forms. 215 SW Adams Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Age 65 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 846-2773 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE ELDER SAFE PROGRAM
